headlines.headline: World Weighs Fate of Mideast Ceasefire After US Seizes Iranian Cargo Ship
headlines.summary: The US military said it fired on an Iranian-flagged cargo ship as the vessel sailed toward Iran's Bandar Abbas port, while Iran's military said the ship had been traveling from China. The seizure throws the fragile ceasefire into question as negotiators head to Pakistan for talks.

headlines.headline: Oil Price Plunge on US-Iran Peace Hopes Gives Indian Markets Some Breather
headlines.summary: The Indian rupee and government bonds will be comforted by a drop in oil prices even as shipping through the Strait of Hormuz remains at a standstill, days before a ceasefire between the United States and Iran is set to expire.

headlines.headline: Wall Street Set to Wade Deeper Into Earnings Season as Stocks Hit Record Highs
headlines.summary: Investors will look to a heavy week of US corporate results to further fuel a stunning rebound in the stock market, which has shaken off war-related concerns to reach record peaks despite ongoing Middle East tensions.

headlines.headline: US Officials Attend IMF Meetings in 'Parallel Reality' as World Grapples With Oil Crisis
headlines.summary: The IMF's spring meetings buzzed with talk of a 13 million barrel per day oil shortfall reshaping economies, rates and defense alliances, yet US officials treated it as a sideshow. The disconnect highlights how counting on US leadership to resolve crises is no longer the guarantee it had long been.

headlines.headline: Trump Issues Iran Ultimatum as Bulgaria Votes and Robots Race Ahead
headlines.summary: US President Donald Trump accused Iran of a 'total violation' of the ceasefire, threatened to wipe out Iran's bridges and power plants, and said his envoys will arrive in Pakistan on Monday for more talks. Meanwhile, Bulgaria's pro-Russian former president appears set for election victory.

headlines.headline: Man Kills 8 Children in Louisiana Mass Shooting, Deadliest US Attack in Two Years
headlines.summary: A gunman killed eight children aged 1 to 14 in domestic-related shootings at two homes in Louisiana before being shot dead by police. It was the deadliest mass shooting in the United States in more than two years, reigniting debates over gun violence.

headlines.headline: Bulgaria's Pro-Russia Former President Radev Wins Landslide Election Victory
headlines.summary: Progress Bulgaria, the Kremlin-friendly coalition led by former President Rumen Radev, is on track to win 37-38% of the vote according to exit polls. The result could end years of weak coalition governments and shift the EU member's foreign policy orientation toward Moscow.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Carries Out First-Ever Shahed Interception Using Naval Drone Platform
headlines.summary: Ukraine's military carried out the first-ever interception of a Russian Shahed drone using a naval drone platform operated by the 412th Nemesis Brigade. The breakthrough demonstrates Kyiv's growing innovation in maritime defense capabilities against Russian aerial threats.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Strikes Russian Oil Refinery in Krasnodar Krai, Major Fire Reported
headlines.summary: Ukrainian drones struck the Tuapse Oil Refinery in southern Russia's Krasnodar Krai during an overnight attack, causing a major fire. Kyiv continues its campaign to disrupt Moscow's war funding by targeting Russian energy infrastructure.

headlines.headline: Memorial Day 2026: 174 IDF Soldiers and 79 Israeli Civilians Killed in Past Year
headlines.summary: Israel's Memorial Day 2026 commemorates 174 IDF soldiers and 79 Israeli civilians killed over the past year, a significant increase reflecting the ongoing multi-front war. The toll underscores the human cost of the Iran war and Gaza conflict.

headlines.headline: UK Police Investigate Iran Links to Arson Attacks on Jewish Sites in London
headlines.summary: British police are investigating possible Iranian links to a series of arson attacks on Jewish targets in London, with the UK chief rabbi warning of a 'sustained campaign of violence' against the Jewish community gathering momentum.

headlines.headline: US Military Strike Kills Three on Alleged Drug Boat in Caribbean
headlines.summary: The US military killed three people in a strike on a boat accused of ferrying drugs in the Caribbean Sea, continuing the Trump administration's campaign against 'narcoterrorism' that has now killed at least 181 people. Critics have questioned the legality of the boat strikes amid continued operations despite the Iran war.

headlines.headline: Canada's Strong US Economic Ties Are Now a Weakness, PM Carney Says
headlines.summary: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said the country's strong economic ties to the United States were once a strength but are now a weakness that must be corrected, citing Trump's tariffs and growing uncertainty. 'Hope isn't a plan and nostalgia is not a strategy,' Carney said, urging Canada to diversify away from US dependence.

headlines.headline: Restoring Oil Flows Through Hormuz Will Take Months or Years Even After War Ends
headlines.summary: Even if the guns fall silent, oil and gas flows through the Strait of Hormuz will take months—possibly years—to recover to pre-war levels. The stop-start shipping traffic underscores profound uncertainty hanging over the world's most critical energy chokepoint.

headlines.headline: US Navy Destroyer Disables Iranian Cargo Ship Breaking Blockade
headlines.summary: The USS Spruance fired on the Iranian-flagged M/V Touska after a six-hour standoff when the vessel attempted to reach Bandar Abbas in violation of the US naval blockade. Marines boarded the ship, which remains in US custody, while Iran vowed retaliation calling it 'armed piracy.'

headlines.headline: Ukraine Unveils New Koral Surface-to-Air Missile for Domestic Air Defense
headlines.summary: Ukraine displayed what appears to be the Koral medium-range surface-to-air missile during a defense industry exhibition, with an estimated range of 100km and anti-ballistic missile capability. The domestically developed SAM could help offset Ukraine's shortage of Western air defense systems.

headlines.headline: Pakistani Journalist Says Iran's Military Strength Delivered Strategic Results
headlines.summary: A senior Pakistani journalist argues Iran achieved strategic gains in its war with the US and Israel, including establishing control over the Strait of Hormuz and potentially accelerating the reduction of the American military footprint in the Middle East. Pakistan mediated the ceasefire and continues hosting negotiations.

headlines.headline: Iran Will Never Cede Control of Strait of Hormuz, Senior Politician Tells BBC
headlines.summary: Iranian lawmaker Ebrahim Azizi told the BBC that Tehran will 'never' give up control of the Strait of Hormuz, calling it an 'inalienable right.' He said Iran is introducing a bill to enshrine its control over the strategic waterway in law, viewing it as a permanent asset rather than just a bargaining chip in negotiations.

headlines.headline: Zelensky Condemns US Extension of Russian Sanctions Waiver Amid Energy Crisis
headlines.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky condemned the US decision to extend a waiver allowing Russia to sell oil already loaded on vessels until May 16. Zelensky said 'every dollar paid for Russian oil is money for the war,' warning that Russia's shadow fleet of 110 tankers would bring $10 billion to Moscow's coffers.

headlines.headline: US Businesses Can Begin Claiming Tariff Refunds Monday After Supreme Court Ruling
headlines.summary: A refund system for businesses that paid tariffs the Supreme Court ruled unconstitutional is set to launch Monday. Over 330,000 importers paid about $166 billion on 53 million shipments, with 56,497 importers eligible for $127 billion in refunds including interest. The process is expected to take 60-90 days per approved claim.

headlines.headline: Asia's Energy Pain Far From Over as LNG Shock Hits World's Factory
headlines.summary: Asian economies are grappling with tightened LNG supply, mounting foreign exchange pressure and damage already locked into Q2 performance as the Iran war disrupts 20% of global oil and LNG flows. The Philippines' GDP growth was downgraded to 3.7% from projected 5-6%, with the IMF warning of 'permanent scarring' across the region.

headlines.headline: India Faces Fertiliser Crisis as Iran War Disrupts Gulf Supplies
headlines.summary: India's urea imports have surged to $935 per tonne from $508 pre-war, with ammonia facilities in Qatar and Saudi Arabia shut after Iranian strikes. Domestic urea production has fallen from 2.5 million tonnes monthly to just 1.5 million tonnes due to LNG supply disruptions, threatening the kharif crop season.

headlines.headline: Australia's Big Banks Brace for Oil Shock Fallout as Hormuz Remains Blocked
headlines.summary: Australia's major lenders Westpac and National Australia Bank are increasing bad loan provisions as the prolonged Hormuz closure begins impacting the economy. The government has announced a $1 billion emergency loan program administered through the big four banks to help businesses weather the fuel crisis.

headlines.headline: North Korea Tests Cluster Warhead Missiles With Kim Jong Un Observing
headlines.summary: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un supervised test-launches of the upgraded Hwasongpho-11 Ra tactical ballistic missile with cluster bomb and fragmentation mine warheads. Five missiles struck a 12.5-13 hectare target area with 'very high density,' demonstrating the regime's continued weapons development amid global distractions.

headlines.headline: Spain Urges EU to End Association Agreement With Israel Over International Law Violations
headlines.summary: Spain announced it will ask the European Union to end its association agreement with Israel over alleged violations of international law. The move marks a significant escalation in European diplomatic pressure on Israel amid the broader Middle East conflict.

headlines.headline: Japan's Taiwan Strait Warship Transit on Treaty Anniversary Raises New Flashpoint With China
headlines.summary: A Japanese warship's transit of the Taiwan Strait on the anniversary of a key security treaty drew sharp criticism from Beijing, which said the passage 'harmed the feelings of the Chinese people.' Tensions in East Asia reached a fresh peak amid competing maritime claims.

headlines.headline: Japan Self-Defense Forces to Join Philippine-US Military Drills in South China Sea
headlines.summary: Japan's Self-Defense Forces will take part in annual military drills led by the Philippines and the US, testing Tokyo's commitment to security in the South China Sea. The exercises come as Japan expands its regional defense partnerships amid growing tensions with China.

headlines.headline: Bahrain Reviews Citizenship Laws After Foiling Iran-Linked Cells
headlines.summary: Bahrain's King Hamad announced measures against those who 'betrayed' the country after authorities foiled Iran-linked cells. The Gulf state is reviewing citizenship laws, potentially stripping nationality from dissidents, amid heightened regional tensions with Iran.

headlines.headline: Two US Embassy Officials Die in Car Crash in Mexico After Drug Operation
headlines.summary: Two US embassy officials died in a car crash in Chihuahua alongside two Mexican agents following a drug operation. The officials were trainers working with Mexican counterparts as part of ongoing bilateral security cooperation efforts.

headlines.headline: US Navy Seizes Iranian Cargo Ship Near Strait of Hormuz, Tehran Vows Response
headlines.summary: The US Navy fired on and seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship near the Strait of Hormuz after a six-hour standoff, with Iran's military calling it 'piracy' and vowing a swift response. The incident throws the fragile ceasefire into question as US negotiators head to Pakistan for talks.

headlines.headline: CBS Reports on Operation to Remove Iran's Highly Enriched Uranium Stockpile
headlines.summary: A 60 Minutes report examines the challenges of securing Iran's estimated 1,000 pounds of 60%-enriched uranium stored deep underground at Isfahan. Experts say the operation would require thousands of US troops and weeks on the ground, with any deal needing Iran's cooperation.

headlines.headline: Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz Again, Says Traffic Control Until War Ends
headlines.summary: Iran has again closed the Strait of Hormuz, reversing a brief reopening and saying it will maintain 'supervision and control' over traffic until the war definitively ends. Iran's chief negotiator stated: 'It is impossible for others to pass through the Strait of Hormuz while we cannot.'

headlines.headline: Belarus Leader Says Meeting With Trump Possible Once 'Big Deal' Is Ready
headlines.summary: Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko said he would be ready to meet US President Donald Trump once a 'big deal' between the two countries has been prepared. The statement signals potential shifts in Belarus-West relations amid the ongoing geopolitical realignment.

headlines.headline: IMF and World Bank Meetings Show Limits in Mitigating Shocks, Reliance on US
headlines.summary: The IMF spring meetings revealed growing global anxiety about a 13 million barrel per day oil shortfall, but US officials treated the energy crisis as a sideshow. The disconnect highlights how counting on US leadership to resolve crises is 'no longer the guarantee it had long been.'

headlines.headline: Iran Replenishing Missile Launchers Faster Than Before War, Guards Commander Says
headlines.summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guards Aerospace Force commander said the country now updates and replenishes its missile and drone launchers at a higher speed than before the war with the US and Israel. The statement signals Iran's continued military capacity despite weeks of strikes.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo Decries Escalation in Ukraine War, Calls for Weapons to Fall Silent
headlines.summary: Pope Leo on Sunday decried the intensification of the war in Ukraine, calling 'for the weapons to fall silent and for the path of dialogue to be followed.' The appeal comes amid Russia's recent mass missile attacks that killed at least 17 civilians.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Pushes Europe to Build Ballistic Missile Defense System
headlines.summary: President Zelenskiy said Europe must have its own defense system against ballistic weapons, with Ukraine already holding talks with several countries on its creation. The push comes as ballistic missile defense remains one of Ukraine's biggest challenges in the war with Russia.

headlines.headline: Pro-Russian Former President Set for Landslide Win in Bulgaria Election
headlines.summary: Pro-Russian former President Rumen Radev is set for a runaway victory in Bulgaria's election and may even secure a parliamentary majority, exit polls showed. The result could end years of weak coalition governments and alter the EU member's foreign policy orientation.

headlines.headline: France and Greece to Renew Defense Pact for Five Years Amid Iran War
headlines.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron will travel to Greece next week to renew a security pact between the two countries for another five years, a Greek government official said. The renewal comes against the backdrop of the war in Iran and shifting European security dynamics.

headlines.headline: Israeli Soldier Filmed Smashing Jesus Statue in Lebanon Sparks Outrage
headlines.summary: An image showing an Israeli soldier using a jackhammer to smash a statue of Jesus in southern Lebanon went viral, sparking outrage including among US conservatives. The incident in the Maronite Christian village of Debel drew condemnation from figures like Marjorie Taylor Greene and Matt Gaetz, potentially damaging Israel's support among evangelical Christians.

headlines.headline: Iran Vows 'Swift Response' After US Seizes Iranian Cargo Ship Near Hormuz
headlines.summary: Iran's military vowed retaliation after US Marines seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship near the Strait of Hormuz. Trump said the Navy 'blew a hole in the engineroom' of the vessel Touska. Meanwhile, Brent crude surged 7% to $96.85 as markets reacted to renewed Hormuz uncertainty.

headlines.headline: Chinese Humanoid Robot Beats Human Record in Half-Marathon by 6 Minutes
headlines.summary: A humanoid robot named Lightning, developed by Chinese company Honor, completed a half-marathon in Beijing in 50 minutes 26 seconds—beating the human world record by over 6 minutes. The race attracted 105 robot competitors, up from 21 last year, showcasing China's rapid advances in humanoid robotics and autonomous navigation.

headlines.headline: Iranian National Charged in US With Trafficking Arms to Sudan for Tehran
headlines.summary: US federal prosecutors charged Iranian national Shamim Mafi with brokering the sale of drones, bombs, and millions of rounds of ammunition manufactured by Iran and sold to Sudan. Mafi, a lawful US resident since 2016, was arrested at Los Angeles International Airport and faces up to 20 years in prison if convicted.

headlines.headline: Ukraine's Long-Range Strikes Inflicted $2.3 Billion in Russian Oil Losses in March
headlines.summary: Ukrainian estimates show long-range strikes on Russian oil infrastructure caused at least $2.3 billion in lost oil revenue for Russia in March alone, Zelensky said. The attacks disabled about 40% of Russia's oil export capacity as Ukraine targets the Kremlin's key revenue source.

headlines.headline: Gulf Equities Mixed as Hormuz Uncertainty Caps Ceasefire Optimism
headlines.summary: Gulf equities ended mixed on Sunday after President Trump said Iran had committed a serious violation of the ceasefire, renewing uncertainty over the Strait of Hormuz and the state of US-Iran talks despite his insistence that a peace deal would happen. Markets remain cautious amid conflicting signals.

headlines.headline: Dollar Rebounds as Middle East Tensions Reignite, Hormuz Closed
headlines.summary: The dollar headed for its highest level in a week at the start of Asian trading as renewed tensions in the Middle East sent investors scurrying for safe havens. Iran's reversal on reopening the Strait of Hormuz sparked fresh risk aversion.

headlines.headline: Oil Jumps, Stock Futures Slip as Iran Tensions Unsettle Markets
headlines.summary: Oil prices jumped, the US dollar rose and stock futures fell as investors dealt with conflicting messages about the Iran war and news that the Strait of Hormuz was closed again. The renewed uncertainty overshadowed ceasefire hopes.

headlines.headline: Kim Jong Un Oversees Test of New Cluster Warhead Missile as North Korea Accelerates Weapons Program
headlines.summary: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un oversaw test launches of upgraded short-range ballistic missiles including the Hwasong-11 Ra equipped with a cluster warhead, state media KCNA reported. The test demonstrates Pyongyang's continued weapons development despite international pressure.

headlines.headline: IMF Cuts 2026 Global Growth Outlook, Warns of Recession Risk From Iran War
headlines.summary: The International Monetary Fund has trimmed its global growth forecast for 2026, warning that a prolonged Iran war could significantly worsen the outlook if energy prices surge further. The revision reflects growing economic uncertainty from the Middle East conflict.

headlines.headline: Michigan Attorney General Rejects Trump Administration Ballot Request in Election Push
headlines.summary: Michigan's attorney general is rejecting an effort by the US Justice Department to obtain ballots and other voting materials, pushing back against what critics call a broader administration effort to challenge election administration.

headlines.headline: Trump Approval Rating Hits Record Low in NBC News Poll as Iran War Drags On
headlines.summary: A new NBC News poll shows the war in Iran is dragging down President Trump's approval rating, with just 37% of American adults approving of his performance. The poll reflects growing public concern about the prolonged conflict.

headlines.headline: Most Voters Blame Trump for Rising Gas Prices, Quinnipiac Poll Shows
headlines.summary: Most registered voters blame President Trump for rising gas prices, according to a new Quinnipiac University national poll. The survey highlights the political cost of the Iran war's impact on energy prices ahead of the midterm elections.

headlines.headline: Oil Prices Rebound 7% as Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz Again
headlines.summary: Oil prices rebounded more than 7% on Monday after tumbling more than 9% on Friday as Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz again, reversing a brief reopening. Brent crude futures jumped $6.56 to $96.94 a barrel as the vital waterway remains blocked.

headlines.headline: EV Sales Soar in Europe as Iran War Drives Petrol Prices to Record Highs
headlines.summary: Sales of fully electric cars in Europe's main auto markets jumped by almost a third in the first quarter of 2026, as drivers sought alternatives to combustion engines after the Iran war caused the highest spike in petrol prices in years.

headlines.headline: Bangladesh Raises Fuel Prices 10-15% as Iran War Drives Up Global Costs
headlines.summary: Bangladesh raised retail fuel prices by 10% to 15%, citing a sharp surge in global crude oil prices and tightening supplies caused by the ongoing Middle East conflict. The energy ministry announced the increases late Saturday as the Iran war continues to ripple through global energy markets.

headlines.headline: Two Foreigners Arrested in Iran for Importing Starlink Technology
headlines.summary: Iranian authorities arrested four individuals, including two foreign nationals, in the country's northwest on charges of being part of a 'U.S.-Israel-linked espionage network' for importing Starlink satellite internet technology. The arrests highlight Iran's continued crackdown on unauthorized communications technology.

headlines.headline: US Energy Chief Warns Gas Prices Could Stay Above $3 Until Next Year
headlines.summary: The US Energy Secretary warned that gasoline prices could remain above $3 per gallon through next year, with the average price at $4.05 per gallon compared to $3.16 a year ago. The Iran war's disruption of global oil supplies continues to pressure American consumers.

headlines.headline: US Officials Attend IMF Meetings in 'Parallel Reality' as World Grapples With Oil Crisis
headlines.summary: IMF spring meetings buzzed with talk of a 13 million barrel per day oil shortfall reshaping economies and defense alliances, but US officials treated the energy crisis as a sideshow. The disconnect highlights growing tensions between American policy and global economic concerns.

headlines.headline: Investors Return to 'TINA' Trades as US-Iran Ceasefire Revives Stock Market Hopes
headlines.summary: The US-Iran ceasefire has revived 'TINA' (There Is No Alternative) stock market trades, driven by peace hopes, soaring US earnings growth, and the relative insulation of the American economy from energy shocks. Investors are piling into US stocks as recession fears ease.

headlines.headline: DHS Shutdown Raises Security Concerns for FIFA World Cup 50 Days Away
headlines.summary: With the FIFA World Cup just over 50 days away, questions are mounting about whether the Department of Homeland Security shutdown will affect security preparations for the tournament. Former DHS officials express concerns about readiness amid political uncertainty.

headlines.headline: Iran War Drives Surge in Southeast Asian Palm Oil Exports
headlines.summary: Demand for crude palm oil from Malaysia and Indonesia has surged as the Iran war drives countries to build stockpiles. However, future production faces constraints from rising fertilizer costs, climate change impacts, and aging palm plantations.

headlines.headline: India Brings 500 MW Sodium-Cooled Nuclear Reactor Online Using Thorium
headlines.summary: India has brought a 500 MW sodium-cooled reactor online at Kalpakkam, leveraging its abundant domestic thorium resources for energy security. The country plans to build 18 standard design reactors as part of a state-led nuclear program with full control over the value chain.

headlines.headline: Japan's Energy Diplomacy Runs Into China's Head Start in Central Asia, Latin America
headlines.summary: As Japan steps up energy diplomacy to secure alternatives to Middle Eastern oil and gas, Tokyo is finding that China has already established a significant lead in Central Asia and Latin America. The competition highlights shifting energy geopolitics as nations seek to diversify away from the volatile Middle East.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo Voices Solidarity With Ukraine as Civilian Attacks Escalate
headlines.summary: Pope Leo XIV expressed solidarity with Ukraine amid escalating attacks against civilians, saying he was 'deeply saddened' by the recent escalation. The Pope renewed his appeal for 'weapons to fall silent' after Russia launched a mass missile attack that killed at least 17 civilians.

headlines.headline: Iranian Official Says Over 20 Passenger Planes Damaged in US-Israeli Attacks
headlines.summary: An Iranian aviation official said more than 20 passenger planes were damaged in US-Israeli attacks, though reports of wider damage were inaccurate as most airport capacity remains intact. The statement comes amid ongoing conflict that has disrupted regional aviation.
